context: Tests whether the agent correctly treats all files as a single logical unit (producing one commit), writes a conventional commit message with proper type, scope, action verb, concise description, and ≤72 character lines — without listing individual file names.

checklist.name: Single commit for one logical unit
checklist.description: commit-log.txt shows exactly one new commit beyond the initial commit (all three files committed together, not split across multiple commits)

checklist.name: Conventional commit type: feat
checklist.description: The commit message uses the `feat` type prefix

checklist.name: Scope present and short
checklist.description: The commit message includes a scope in parentheses that is a short logical area name (e.g., `users`, `user-management`, `dashboard`) — not a file path

checklist.name: Action verb starts description
checklist.description: The commit description (after the colon) starts with an action verb such as add, implement, introduce, create, or similar

checklist.name: No file names in message
checklist.description: The commit message does NOT mention individual file names such as users_migration.sql, users.js, or UserList.tsx

checklist.name: Describes intent, not implementation
checklist.description: The commit message describes what the change achieves (e.g., 'add user management') rather than how it is implemented (e.g., 'create SQL migration and React component')

checklist.name: Line length ≤ 72 chars
checklist.description: Every line of the commit message is ≤ 72 characters long

checklist.name: Correct conventional format
checklist.description: Commit message matches the pattern `<type>(<scope>): <description>` on the first line

checklist.name: No layer enumeration in scope
checklist.description: The commit scope does NOT enumerate technical layers (e.g., NOT `api+frontend+db` or `api,db,ui`) — scope names a feature area, not a list of layers

checklist.name: git log captured
checklist.description: commit-log.txt exists and contains git log --oneline output

checklist.name: Message is one line
checklist.description: The commit message is a single-line subject (no body/footer needed for a single logical unit change)
